01_initial_request.input_data: genetically encoded proximity-labeling enzyme fused to a protein or localization signal peptide
01_initial_request.user_intent: dissect the complex cellular network at the molecular level in the plant field

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Proximity labeling (PL) requires an engineered biotin ligase to tag proximal proteins. [Gap] Traditional ligases like BioID have slow kinetics and low signal-to-noise. [Decision] To achieve increased signal-to-noise ratio, chose to use TurboID to perform enzyme selection for high-activity labeling, expecting to obtain efficient biotinylation in minutes.

parameters.activity: highest activity of all biotin ligases

02_agent_trajectory.observation: TurboID allows for highly efficient labeling in a timescale of minutes and a broader range of working temperatures.

references.citation: Branon TC, et al. 2018. Efficient proximity labeling in living cells and organisms with TurboID. Nat. Biotechnol. 36:880–87 (Ref 10)
references.relevance: Describes the engineering of high-activity TurboID.

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] The PL enzyme must be targeted to a specific protein complex or organelle. [Gap] Improper fusion can interfere with bait function. [Decision] To ensure physiological relevance, chose to use Genetic Engineering to perform fusion of PL enzyme to bait protein or localization signal peptide, expecting to obtain functional bait-PL fusion constructs.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: Bait protein is fused to a PL enzyme and expressed under the bait protein's native promoter at the endogenous level.

references.citation: Mair A, et al. 2019. Proximity labeling of protein complexes and cell-type-specific organellar proteomes in Arabidopsis enabled by TurboID. eLife 8:e47864 (Ref 72)
references.relevance: Investigates TurboID applications in plants including construct design.

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Biotin is required as a substrate for the ligase to label proximal proteins. [Gap] Excess free biotin competes with streptavidin binding during enrichment. [Decision] To optimize labeling while minimizing background, chose to use Biotin to perform treatment on plant tissues, expecting to obtain biotinylated proximal proteins.

parameters.duration: ≤ 1 h for abundant proteins, up to several hours for less-abundant bait proteins

02_agent_trajectory.observation: Biotinylated proteins are generated within the 10-nm radius of the PL enzyme.

references.citation: Mair A, et al. 2019. Proximity labeling of protein complexes and cell-type-specific organellar proteomes in Arabidopsis enabled by TurboID. eLife 8:e47864 (Ref 72)
references.relevance: Provides optimization parameters for biotin treatment in plants.

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Biotinylated proteins need to be isolated from the complex proteome. [Gap] Traditional affinity purification loses weak/transient interactors. [Decision] To capture biotinylated proteins under harsh conditions, chose to use Streptavidin beads to perform affinity capture, expecting to obtain enriched biotinylated proteins.

parameters.conditions: high concentrations of detergent, salt, or denaturing agents

02_agent_trajectory.observation: Biotinylated proteins are captured by streptavidin beads under very harsh conditions.

references.citation: Roux KJ, et al. 2012. A promiscuous biotin ligase fusion protein identifies proximal and interacting proteins in mammalian cells. J. Cell Biol. 196:801–10 (Ref 99)
references.relevance: Establishes the use of streptavidin beads for proximity labeling enrichment.

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Proteins must be digested into peptides for MS analysis. [Gap] Elution of intact proteins from streptavidin is challenging. [Decision] To elute and prepare samples for MS, chose to use Trypsin to perform on-bead digestion, expecting to obtain peptide mixtures.

parameters.duration: ~3-h short digestion followed by overnight full digestion

02_agent_trajectory.observation: Biotinylated proteins are eluted from the beads using trypsin digestion.

references.citation: Annual Review of Plant Biology Proximity Labeling in Plants, Section 5.1
references.relevance: Describes the workflow of protein-level enrichment including on-bead digestion.

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Peptides need to be identified and quantified. [Gap] Complex mixtures require high sensitivity. [Decision] To analyze the peptide mixture, chose to use LC-MS/MS to perform mass spectrometric analysis, expecting to obtain spectral data for protein identification.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: Peptides are analyzed via liquid chromatography–coupled tandem mass spectrometry.

references.citation: Annual Review of Plant Biology Proximity Labeling in Plants, Section 6
references.relevance: Discusses quantification strategies requiring high-resolution instruments.

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Raw MS data needs processing for quantification. [Gap] Variance between runs needs normalization. [Decision] To quantify protein abundance, chose to use MaxQuant to perform label-free quantification (LFQ), expecting to obtain relative protein abundance values.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: Protein abundance is quantified using the sum of XICs of peptides.

references.citation: Cox J, Mann M. 2008. MaxQuant enables high peptide identification rates... Nat. Biotechnol. 26:1367–74 (Ref 24)
references.relevance: Describes the MaxQuant software for proteome-wide protein quantification.

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Candidates from PL-MS need biological validation. [Gap] MS data alone does not confirm direct interaction. [Decision] To validate direct protein-protein interactions, chose to use Bimolecular fluorescence complementation (BiFC) to perform interaction assay, expecting to obtain confirmation of PPIs.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: Direct PPIs are validated using BiFC and yeast two-hybrid assays.

references.citation: Mair A, et al. 2019. Proximity labeling of protein complexes and cell-type-specific organellar proteomes in Arabidopsis enabled by TurboID. eLife 8:e47864 (Ref 72)
references.relevance: Uses BiFC to validate candidates from PL-MS experiments.

03_success_verification.validation_technique: Comparison of TurboID performance against traditional BioID and AP-MS methods

signal_to_noise_ratio.interpretation: TurboID enables spatiotemporal mapping with a dramatically increased signal-to-noise ratio compared to BioID.

labeling_time.interpretation: TurboID allows for highly efficient labeling in a timescale of minutes versus 18-24 h for BioID.

affinity_capture.interpretation: Streptavidin affinity (Kd=10^-14 mol/L) allows capture under harsh conditions, reducing contaminants.

03_success_verification.final_verdict: The research objective to dissect the complex cellular network with increased signal-to-noise ratio using biotin ligase-based PL-MS is achieved through the adoption of TurboID and optimized PL-MS workflows described in the review.
